hypothesis: Short-term price momentum in KOSPI is persistent over 20 trading days, as institutional herding amplifies trending behaviour.
Volatility-adjusted momentum (Sharpe-style) better predicts KOSPI returns than raw momentum by filtering noise-driven moves.
Volume-price divergence (high volume with flat price) predicts KOSPI reversals at the 5-day horizon.
Mean-reversion after RSI extremes in KOSPI large-caps produces reliable short-term alpha.
High-low range expansion relative to recent average signals KOSPI breakouts with continued momentum.
